Fault-adjacent mutants are those that, when changed, cause nearby functions or methods to malfunction, often due to incorrect logic flow or missing conditions.

In this case, changing line 381 from checking if (isFixed()) to always being true could have bypassed necessary checks for certain time zones, leading to the failure of tests expecting correct behavior in Berlin and New York. These tests likely rely on the isLocalDateTimeGap method's proper functioning, which was affected by the mutation.
